name: Makar Sankranti
description: Harvest festival celebrating the sun's transition into Capricorn.
history: One of the few ancient Indian festivals observed according to solar cycles.
traditions: Flying kites, eating sesame and jaggery sweets, holy river dips.

name: Pongal
description: Multi-day Tamil harvest festival.
history: Dedicated to the Sun God, Surya.
traditions: Boiling new rice in milk, decorating cows, making kolams.

name: Onam
description: Annual harvest festival in the state of Kerala.
history: Commemorates the legendary King Mahabali.
traditions: Pookkalam (flower carpets), Vallam Kali (boat races), Onasadya feast.

name: Bihu
description: A set of three important Assamese festivals.
history: Rooted in agricultural cycles of Assam.
traditions: Folk dancing, feasts, lighting bonfires (Meji).

name: Lohri
description: Popular winter folk festival celebrated primarily in Punjab.
history: Marks the passing of the winter solstice.
traditions: Lighting massive bonfires, dancing Bhangra, eating popcorn and peanuts.

name: Raksha Bandhan
description: Celebration of the bond between brothers and sisters.
history: Ancient tradition denoting protection.
traditions: Tying rakhi (sacred thread), exchanging gifts and sweets.

name: Ganesh Chaturthi
description: Festival celebrating the birth of Lord Ganesha.
history: Popularized by Shivaji and later by Lokmanya Tilak.
traditions: Installing clay idols, eating modaks, vibrant processions for immersion (Visarjan).

name: Durga Puja
description: Reveres the goddess Durga.
history: Marks Durga's victory over the shape-shifting demon Mahishasura.
traditions: Elaborate pandals (temporary shrines), cultural performances, Dhunuchi dance.

name: Dussehra
description: Celebrates the triumph of good over evil.
history: Marks Rama's victory over Ravana and Durga's victory over Mahishasura.
traditions: Burning huge effigies of Ravana, Ramlila performances.

name: Karwa Chauth
description: Fasting festival by married Hindu women for the safety and longevity of their husbands.
history: Traditional fasting ritual originating in Northern India.
traditions: Strict fasting from sunrise to moonrise, dressing in bridal colors.

name: Maha Shivaratri
description: The Great Night of Shiva.
history: Celebrates the overcoming of darkness and ignorance.
traditions: All-night vigils, fasting, offering milk and bael leaves to the Shiva Lingam.

name: Janmashtami
description: Celebrates the birth of Lord Krishna.
history: Marks the 8th avatar of Vishnu.
traditions: Midnight prayers, Dahi Handi (breaking butter pots), fasting.

name: Gudi Padwa
description: Spring-time festival that marks the traditional new year for Marathi and Konkani Hindus.
history: Linked to the mythical day on which Brahma created time and universe.
traditions: Raising a Gudi (decorated flag), eating sweet-bitter neem leaves.

name: Ugadi
description: New Year's Day for the states of Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, and Karnataka.
history: Follows the Hindu lunisolar calendar.
traditions: Creating colorful rangolis, eating Ugadi Pachadi (a special mixture of 6 tastes).

name: Pushkar Camel Fair
description: An annual multi-day livestock fair and cultural fete.
history: One of India's largest camel, horse and cattle fairs.
traditions: Trading livestock, mustache competitions, hot air ballooning.

name: Hornbill Festival
description: Festival of festivals showcasing the rich culture of Nagaland.
history: Organized by the State Tourism and Art & Culture Departments.
traditions: Tribal dances, Morung exhibitions, Naga wrestling.

name: Rann Utsav
description: A carnival of music, dance, and natural beauty in the White Desert.
history: Promotes the culture and heritage of the Kutch region.
traditions: Full moon night viewing of the white salt desert, cultural programs.

name: Hemis Festival
description: A colorful celebration marking the birth anniversary of Guru Padmasambhava.
history: Held at the Hemis Monastery in Ladakh.
traditions: Cham dance (masked dance) performed by monks.

name: Thrissur Pooram
description: The most famous of all poorams in Kerala.
history: Started by Sakthan Thampuran, the Maharaja of Cochin in 1798.
traditions: Massive elephant procession, traditional percussion ensembles (Panchavadyam).

name: Rath Yatra
description: A major Hindu festival associated with Lord Jagannath.
history: One of the oldest chariot festivals in the world.
traditions: Pulling massive wooden chariots carrying deities.

name: Chhath Puja
description: Ancient Hindu festival dedicated to the Sun God (Surya).
history: Vedic festival originating in the Indian subcontinent.
traditions: Holy bathing in rivers, fasting, offering prayers to the rising and setting sun.

name: Baisakhi
description: Historical and religious festival in Sikhism.
history: Commemorates the formation of the Khalsa Panth by Guru Gobind Singh in 1699.
traditions: Nagar kirtans (processions), community meals (Langar), Bhangra dancing.

name: Desert Festival Jaisalmer
description: Annual festival held amidst the sand dunes of the Thar Desert.
history: Organized to showcase the rich and colorful culture of Rajasthan.
traditions: Turban tying competitions, camel polo, fire dancing.

name: Khajuraho Dance Festival
description: Festival of classical dances held against the backdrop of Khajuraho temples.
history: Started in 1975 to promote Indian classical dance.
traditions: Kathak, Bharatanatyam, Odissi, Kuchipudi, Manipuri, and Kathakali performances.

name: Goa Carnival
description: The only carnival in India, bringing the streets of Goa alive.
history: Introduced by the Portuguese, blending with local culture.
traditions: Colorful parades, crowning of King Momo, masked dances.

name: Mysore Dasara
description: State festival of Karnataka (Nadahabba).
history: Celebrated grandly for over 400 years by the Wadiyar dynasty.
traditions: Illuminating the Mysore Palace, Jamboo Savari (elephant procession).

name: Tarnetar Fair
description: A vibrant celebration of ethnic Gujarat's folk-dance, music, and arts.
history: Centered around the Trinetreshwar Mahadev temple.
traditions: Matchmaking for tribal youth, beautifully embroidered umbrellas.

name: Sonepur Mela
description: One of the largest cattle fairs in Asia.
history: Originates from ancient times when kings bought elephants and horses.
traditions: Trading elephants, cattle, and birds, holy dip at the confluence of rivers.

name: Surajkund Crafts Mela
description: International crafts fair showcasing traditional arts and handicrafts.
history: Organized annually in Faridabad, Haryana.
traditions: Exhibiting handlooms, regional cuisines, folk performances.

name: Elephanta Festival
description: A two-day festival of classical dance and music.
history: Held at the UNESCO World Heritage Site of Elephanta Caves.
traditions: Classical Indian performances under the stars with rock-cut caves as a backdrop.

name: Bathukamma
description: Floral festival celebrated predominantly by the women of Telangana.
history: Represents the cultural spirit of Telangana.
traditions: Arranging seasonal flowers in seven concentric layers in the shape of a temple gopuram.

name: Losar
description: Tibetan New Year, celebrated in Ladakh, Sikkim, and Arunachal Pradesh.
history: Pre-Buddhist Bon festival that evolved into a Buddhist celebration.
traditions: Metho ceremonies to drive away evil spirits, offering Khapse.

name: Chapchar Kut
description: The biggest and most important festival of Mizoram.
history: Celebrates the completion of clearing jungles for cultivation.
traditions: Cheraw (bamboo dance), traditional feasts, folk music.
